audit_items.proof_trace: md5.c contains no null guards by design — enforcement is delegated entirely to callers, which is standard C library convention. All callers in the codebase pass stack-allocated struct addresses (&MD5context, &ctx) that cannot be NULL, and digest buffers are always stack arrays. The one borderline path (cred->bv_val via passwd.c) is always paired with bv_len=0 per OpenLDAP's berval invariant, causing only zero-length memcpy calls (no actual pointer dereference).
